commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance or tuning of the intentional 16 MiB fail-closed boundary and sibling PR sequencing, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff hardens an availability-sensitive provider stream path and does not add dependencies, lockfiles, CI permissions, secret handling, or third-party code execution. (sha=3407ee1021556bcf509c30de98dfd04dd6135c23)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an internal SSE byte guard, applies a 16 MiB cap to Anthropic provider streaming success-body reads, exports the iterator for tests, and adds overflow-cancellation coverage.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +37. Total +144 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main reads and appends Content-Length-less SSE chunks in `iterateSseMessages` with no byte cap, and the PR body includes live local-server output showing the before path consuming 64 MiB while the after path stops near the 16 MiB cap.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance or tuning of the intentional 16 MiB fail-closed boundary and sibling PR sequencing, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(anthropic): bound streaming 200 success-body SSE reads at 16 MiB (provider path)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` (`iterateSseMessages`, the legacy provider SSE parser) accumulates an unbounded SSE byte stream into a string buffer while waiting for line delimiters. A hostile or malfunctioning Anthropic-compatible endpoint (including an MITM-able proxy or a hijacked self-hosted provider) can return a `Content-Length`-less SSE body that streams forever, exhausting process memory on a code path that runs for every legacy-provider Anthropic streaming call.

This is the streaming-body analogue of the bounded-read sweep Alix-007 finished for non-streaming `response.json()`. Same 16 MiB cap, same helper-driven design (`createSseByteGuard`), but on the chunk-by-chunk SSE path that the non-streaming sweep did not cover.

## Changes

- `src/agents/streaming-byte-guard.ts` (NEW, 87 lines) — `createSseByteGuard` helper wrapping a `ReadableStreamDefaultReader` with a byte-level cap. Cancel-on-overflow with `onOverflow` callback producing the error. Core-internal (no SDK surface change).
- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts:347-362` — `iterateSseMessages` wraps `body.getReader()` in `createSseByteGuard` with `ANTHROPIC_MESSAGES_SUCCESS_BODY_MAX_BYTES = 16 * 1024 * 1024`. Function re-exported as `iterateSseMessagesForTest` for direct test access.
- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts` — 1 regression test for the capped read.

## Real behavior proof

Drives production `createSseByteGuard` over a real `node:http` server (127.0.0.1, chunked transfer-encoding, no Content-Length). Covers AFTER (capped), happy path, and BEFORE (unbounded OOM vector). Node v22.22.0, Linux x86_64.

```
=== AFTER (16 MiB cap via createSseByteGuard) ===

  PASS  hostile 64 MiB SSE body: overflow thrown at 16 MiB cap
  PASS  happy path: small 2 KiB SSE body drained without overflow

=== BEFORE (unbounded SSE read — OOM vector) ===

  unbounded read consumed 64.0 MiB — OOM vector confirmed
  PASS  (negative control) unbounded read exceeded 16 MiB cap

=== All anthropic provider bounded-read assertions: 3/3 passed ===
```

- **Evidence after fix**: 3/3 assertions pass. AFTER: 64 MiB hostile SSE body triggers overflow at 16 MiB cap, 2 KiB happy path drains cleanly. BEFORE: unbounded read consumes full 64.0 MiB (OOM vector confirmed — 4× the cap).
- **Behavior addressed**: unbounded SSE buffering in `iterateSseMessages`; after the fix the provider-path streaming read is capped at 16 MiB via `createSseByteGuard`.
- **Real environment tested**: real `node:http` server (127.0.0.1, no Content-Length, chunked SSE) driving production `createSseByteGuard`. Node v22.22.0.
- **Negative control**: unbounded read consumed 64.0 MiB vs capped read stopped at ~16 MiB — cap is load-bearing.
- **What was not tested**: live Anthropic API call (same production helper against same attack shape); cross-platform Node differences.

## Out of scope (separate PRs, sequenced)

- `src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.ts` (`parseSSE`, ChatGPT Responses SSE parser) — covered by sibling PR #96772.
- `src/agents/runtime/proxy.ts` (`streamProxy`, internal proxy SSE parser) — covered by sibling PR #96782.
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts` and `extensions/ollama/src/{setup,stream}.ts` — need `createSseByteGuard` promoted to plugin-SDK first (separate PR).

## Risk

- **Low**: same 16 MiB cap used by `readProviderJsonResponse` for non-streaming Anthropic success bodies. Normal-sized responses unchanged — only the unbounded case throws a clear overflow error instead of OOM.
- **Helper**: core-internal (`src/agents/streaming-byte-guard.ts`); no SDK surface change, no API-baseline regen needed.
- **Cancel propagation**: `createSseByteGuard.cancel(reason)` propagates overflow error to the underlying reader.

Sibling PRs: #96772 (openai-chatgpt SSE), #96782 (proxy SSE), #96777 (chutes-oauth JSON). Together these close the bounded-read gap for the Anthropic + OpenAI + proxy + Chutes surfaces.

AI-assisted: implemented and proof-tested with AI assistance; reviewed by a human before submission.

## Diff stats

```
3 files changed, 146 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
```
target.author: wangmiao0668000666
